title: elaeocarpus poilanei
intro: Elaeocarpus poilanei, also known as the Polillano Tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Elaeocarpaceae, native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Elaeocarpus poilanei include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and pneumonia. Elaeocarpus poilanei is traditionally prepared as a decoction or infusion, where the bark, leaves, or roots are boiled in water to create a herbal remedy for the treatment of various ailments.
